workflow-flight-recorder.description: Native inventory, explicit import, constrained analysis, and focused Python coverage

review-and-fix-contract.description: Focused review-and-fix iteration, guardrail, convergence, telemetry, and prompt-composition contracts

create-issue-contract.description: Focused create-issue Step 3.6 audit, state-owner cutover, authoring-discipline, and revision-delta contracts

cloud_mappings.comment: Trusted cloud workflow/job -> DevFlow consumer mapping, read ONLY by the verification-launch baseline analyzer's cloud census eligibility (issue #527). load_registry ignores this additive section, so the top-level schema_version stays 1 and the recorder is unaffected. A census job is eligible only when its (workflow_file, job) matches an entry below AND the census records scheduled/started agent-step evidence (a skipped job, which never ran its agent step, is ineligible); any job whose (workflow_file, job) is not listed below is ineligible by omission. DELIBERATE OMISSION, recorded so an omission-by-oversight is distinguishable from an omission-by-intent (PR #531 review, completeness-critic): .github/workflows/matcher-probe.yml also runs claude-code-action (in its probe / implement-probe / schedulewakeup-probe / execfile-shape-probe / hook-probe jobs) and is deliberately absent — it is repo-internal permission-probe infrastructure, is not shipped by install.sh, and runs no DevFlow consumer lifecycle, so its runs are not verification-launch subjects. It is the ONLY claude-code-action workflow excluded on purpose; an agent job appearing in .github/workflows/ that is neither listed below nor named here is an oversight, not a carve-out. agent_step is the step ID (the `Run Claude Code` step's `id: claude`) that runs the claude-code-action agent in each listed workflow (informational only; census eligibility keys on job-level scheduled/started status, not the step name, because the census snapshot is job-level). Job values are the Actions jobs-API NAMES: a job that calls a reusable workflow surfaces its nested jobs as 'caller-job / called-job' (devflow-review.yml's review job calls devflow-runner.yml, so its agent job reports as 'review / run'), and devflow-runner.yml itself is workflow_call-only — it never appears as its own workflow run, so it carries no entry here (PR #531 review). WAVE-1 CONSUMER APPROXIMATION: devflow.yml's 'command' job multiplexes /devflow:review, /devflow:review-and-fix, and /devflow:pr-description; because the census snapshot is job-level, all of its runs are attributed to consumer='review'/routed_command='/devflow:review' as a Wave-1 approximation — downstream stratification must not treat this consumer attribution as exact for the command job (PR #531 review).
